doc: BSH-HMS Phase 2 — Migration Baseline & Verified End-State
purpose: Ground-truth snapshot captured BEFORE and AFTER applying Supabase migrations 37/38/39 + the bsh-demo tenant_config row. Every post-application number was re-verified live against the database; see verification_labels.
generated_by: Claude Code autonomous session (BSH-HMS finish-everything-no-push mission)

mechanism_correction.premise_in_task: Apply migrations via Supabase REST exec_sql / POST sql endpoint (PostgREST).
mechanism_correction.finding: FALSE PREMISE — disproven by direct probe.
evidence: POST to PostgREST /rest/v1/rpc/exec_sql returned HTTP 404 — the exec_sql RPC does not exist in this project.
PostgREST cannot execute DDL (CREATE TABLE / CREATE POLICY / CREATE TRIGGER) regardless of RPC; it only proxies row-level CRUD.
mechanism_correction.mechanism_actually_used: Supabase Management API — POST https://api.supabase.com/v1/projects/fncfbywkemsxwuiowxxe/database/query (authenticated with SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN from .env, len 44). This is the authorized arbitrary-SQL channel and the only one that can run DDL here.
management_api_quirks_discovered: Caps query body size (~2KB) — full migration files returned HTTP 413 Payload Too Large. Worked around by splitting each file into individual statements (dollar-quote-aware splitter) and sending them one at a time.

Rejects explicit BEGIN/COMMIT transaction control — returned HTTP 400. Dropped the BEGIN/COMMIT lines; safe because every statement is idempotent (IF NOT EXISTS / CREATE OR REPLACE / DROP ... IF EXISTS).

Requires UTF-8 byte-encoded request body — PowerShell 5.1 Invoke-WebRequest mangled non-ASCII (em-dash) characters, producing HTTP 400 on the statements containing em-dashes (migration 38 COMMENT ON VIEW; migration 39 CREATE TABLE inline comments). Fixed by encoding the body via [System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetBytes() + ContentType 'application/json; charset=utf-8', then re-applying idempotently.

multi_tenant_regression.verdict: PASS — no existing-tenant data touched; no test rows left behind.

industry_gate_proof.captured_label: VERIFIED-DB (negative direction = actual rejection observed; positive direction = INFERRED, gate condition satisfied, not insert-tested to honor no-DELETE guardrail)

negative_test_cosmique_into_bsh_clinical.error_message: bsh_clinical_log is restricted to healthcare_hospital tenants. tenant_id=cosmique has industry=healthcare_clinic

positive_direction_bsh_demo.label: INFERRED
positive_direction_bsh_demo.basis: bsh-demo industry=healthcare_hospital satisfies the trigger's NOT EXISTS(... industry='healthcare_hospital') guard; bsh-demo also appears in hospital_tenants view. Insert path not exercised to avoid leaving test rows (no-DELETE guardrail).

safety_decisions.subscription_status_inactive: bsh-demo was inserted with subscription_status='inactive' (NOT in the task spec, which would have defaulted to 'active'). Reason: prevents the demo tenant being swept into active multi-tenant scheduled loops (§7 multi-tenant loop pattern selects WHERE subscription_status='active') before AMD demo day. Industry triggers gate on industry NOT subscription_status, so hospital-table access still works. User flips to 'active' on demo day.
safety_decisions.no_destructive_sql: No DROP/DELETE/TRUNCATE against existing data. All DDL idempotent + additive. Negative gate tests produced zero rows (trigger fired BEFORE INSERT).
